tweets.text: In 1963 as pioneers of music diplomacy and one of the original jazz ambassadors, Duke Ellington and his orchestra traveled to India to play a concert. More than 60 years later, the next generation of artists continues to be moved and impacted by their music. #InternationalJazzDay https://t.co/RpDVu5pQdR

We are determined to do everything we can to bring an end to the terrible human suffering that we’re seeing every single day in Gaza among children, women, men, who have been caught in a terrible crossfire of Hamas’s making. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/DndMa1fKHB
The designs that Vladimir Putin had on Ukraine to begin with – to erase it from the map, to subsume it into Russia so that it no longer existed – that’s failed. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/A1rIMJXb7y

There are two paths forward for the region as a whole. Today’s Gulf Cooperation Council meeting shows that many more of us want to pursue that affirmative path, and I’m grateful to our colleagues from the GCC for working in partnership to advance in that direction. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/UhtYrVmr2X
.@SecBlinken participates in a conversation with World Economic Forum President Børge Brende in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia. https://t.co/ZH44iRPFoP https://t.co/napKQbcgp4

When students participate in exchanges, they deepen knowledge inside and outside the classroom and forge ties with their peers.

As @SecBlinken noted, we're determined to strengthen our people-to-people ties with China and also increase the number of Americans studying in China. https://t.co/WiH7kmojTT
This week, @SecBlinken traveled to Shanghai and Beijing. We also released the Human Rights Report. https://t.co/ZmaV6CUjBV

In honor of Earth Day earlier this week, several of our U.S. embassies and consulates across the world led clean up efforts in their communities. Together we can help combat climate change and plastic pollution. https://t.co/scxOIKLMl2
Support for Ukraine is not just from the United States, it’s from country after country in Europe and well beyond. https://t.co/iQEybOVEgW
Ukraine stands for values we all share: democracy, self-determination, freedom. We are #UnitedWithUkraine. https://t.co/qfVTbdCfLy

The U.S. and the People’s Republic of China are resuming cooperation:

•counternarcotics

•restarting our military-to-military conversations

•looking together at the future of artificial intelligence

•further strengthening our people-to-people connections. https://t.co/Tfji0MYzmi
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Beijing, People’s Republic of China. https://t.co/JBdT5cG0cF https://t.co/Q1sGY1I51o

.@SecBlinken is in the People's Republic of China to responsibly manage the U.S.-China relationship and discuss a range of bilateral, regional, and global issues that matter to Americans and the world. https://t.co/aoi5C3TOQU

Building on the leaders' commitments in California last fall, @SecBlinken is heading to Shanghai and Beijing to advance progress on key issues — from disrupting the flow of synthetic drugs to establishing talks on artificial intelligence risks and safety. https://t.co/VYXohrKptm

"The U.S. has been leading efforts to get humanitarian assistance into Gaza to alleviate the suffering of innocent Palestinians who have nothing to do with, indeed are victims themselves, of Hamas and its brutal rule." -Special Envoy Satterfield https://t.co/bVQDuy9iIm

David Satterfield, who leads U.S. coordination for humanitarian aid in the Middle East, provides an update on our efforts to facilitate the flow of food, medical supplies, and other aid into Gaza. https://t.co/2jTUVkDgDv
The people who live in Gaza continue to face a horrific humanitarian situation. Humanitarian assistance must be a priority and it must be sustained. https://t.co/lfd8wTiouV

We have a clearer picture of human rights conditions as they are, as well as a renewed determination to strengthen them for the future. — @SecBlinken on the Human Rights Report https://t.co/stuUZw3zff

How much do you know about efforts to curb plastic use and pollution? Take our quiz to find out. 

Together we can protect our planet from mismanaged plastic waste.  https://t.co/qbYi1OnC2g
Happy Passover to all who are celebrating, including our Jewish colleagues serving at home and around the world. Chag Pesach sameach! https://t.co/IV6L2jXLbC

.@SecBlinken released the Human Rights Report today, documenting the status of human rights in nearly 200 countries and territories. “Countries that respect human rights are more likely to be peaceful, prosperous, stable.” https://t.co/IrNBtr4wgJ
“Standing up for freedom and human rights is simply the right thing to do.” -@SecBlinken on the progress made by countries around the world https://t.co/3HYKtbXetp
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks on the release of the Country Reports on Human Rights Practices, followed by the Department Press Briefing. https://t.co/GWan5VYoZO https://t.co/VNLzhZs8Ng

Actions big and small make an impact on the health of our planet. In celebration of Earth Day, #StateOnTheStreet asked people about their green habits. Let us know in the comments, what’s the greenest thing you do? https://t.co/zEfTtpX6mU

.@NASA’s Aqua satellite captured this natural-color image of swirls and patches of possible oil slicks in the Gulf of Mexico in 2010. Mapping oil spills helps define the extent of the problem. #EarthDay https://t.co/Xkdyxud5Kv
.@NASA’s Aqua satellite captured haze-filled images depicting widespread crop fires in northern Pakistan and India in November 2023. #EarthDay https://t.co/1cYsVVqR0S
In many coastal areas, sea levels are rising and land is sinking. This image depicts rapid sinking in Charleston, South Carolina, from 2007 to 2020. #EarthDay https://t.co/OnrA9LkTnP
Examining trends in air pollution holds us accountable. This map shows satellite data of nitrogen dioxide levels in 2016. #EarthDay https://t.co/9JMRf8WDhK
2023 was the hottest year on record. Maps and data help scientists figure out why. #EarthDay https://t.co/FvRTVuHUQw
Satellite imagery and AI uncovered 75% untracked vessels. This crucial data combats illegal fishing and helps conservation. #EarthDay https://t.co/SdcEeJpzq3

From extreme weather to rising sea levels, environmental issues affect us all.

Here’s how satellite imagery is being used to measure, inform, and combat harmful climate effects and protect the planet for generations to come. #EarthDay https://t.co/S6BEWY1b0U

One of the realities that we have to confront is that we are operating in an incredibly diverse world. The greatest strength that we bring to that is our own diversity. We want to make sure that we have a government and we have a Department that fully reflects that diversity. https://t.co/glmizTFvpA

This week at State, we turned ambition into action at the Our Ocean Conference and @SecBlinken and G7 Foreign Ministers are more united than ever in facing critical challenges following meetings in Italy. https://t.co/4XoC06H3sz

The @NatlParkService works every day to protect the national treasure that is our national parks, but the climate crisis is threatening our national parks in new ways. We must solve these evolving issues together. https://t.co/RwzlfCF1if

.@SecBlinken underscored that @G7 Foreign Ministers are more united than ever in facing critical challenges that lie before the international community, including Russia's aggression against Ukraine, the conflict in the Middle East, and supporting a free and open Indo-Pacific. https://t.co/HtYeUgBKEk

It’s imperative that in this moment Ukraine gets more resources that it needs to deal with the ongoing Russian aggression.
Allies and partners, including the @G7 countries, are committed to delivering on that. -@SecBlinken https://t.co/iW14WSURCO

The @G7 is unwavering in its support for Ukraine against Russian aggression.

Putin thinks that he can outwait Ukraine and outwait Ukraine’s supporters. The message coming out of Capri is: He can’t. https://t.co/wcdHQbOzy0

Houthi attacks in the Red Sea have increased the cost of food, medicine, and fuel. Tim Lenderking, the Special Envoy for Yemen, explains the global impacts of these attacks and how the U.S. is working with our global partners to address them. https://t.co/cSn0h0Gvlr

The @G7 remains focused on Gaza and addressing the crisis in the Middle East. We urge the rapid implementation of Israel’s humanitarian aid commitments, and continue efforts to reach a ceasefire deal with the release of hostages. https://t.co/NJbGPmfIZH
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability at the conclusion of the @G7 Foreign Ministers Meeting in Capri, Italy. https://t.co/R169Civf1t https://t.co/5LOhYqu5Jo

Following the #OurOcean Conference in Greece, the U.S. is turning ambition into action, announcing more than $508 million to protect our ocean and help accomplish the goals we outlined on this thread. Want to know more? Visit: https://t.co/z6pE2jENCD

This is a critical time for Ukraine. The Ukrainian people have fought incredibly bravely to defend their country, to recapture territory seized by Russia since February 2022, and, at the same time, to work to build a strong country for the future. https://t.co/ltE8bGM3dV

Why does the U.S. care about the ocean? Our ocean feeds us, sustains us, protects us — and it's at risk. Learn how the United States is addressing the challenges facing our ocean today. https://t.co/z6pE2jFlsb https://t.co/lfaHdnr9Q6

In November 2021, @POTUS launched the President’s Emergency Plan for Adaptation and Resilience. PREPARE seeks to help more than half a billion people in developing countries adapt to and manage the impacts of climate change. More: https://t.co/38ho7SRRZV

Illegal, unreported, and unregulated fishing damages ocean ecosystems and hurts communities that depend on fisheries for food. To counter these threats, the U.S. Maritime Security & Fisheries Enforcement Act brings a whole-of-government approach to building sustainable fisheries and fighting #IUUFishing around the world.

Nearly 40 countries are now collaborating on opportunities and challenges in the Partnership for Atlantic Cooperation. Preventing and responding to abandoned and lost fishing gear is one example of how we are working together with neighbors to share best practices and expertise. https://t.co/rxdQwkrg3c

The U.S. launched the Ocean Conservation Pledge, joined by 24 countries, committing to conserve 30% of our lands and waters by 2030. The pledge catalyzes global efforts to enhance marine conservation. https://t.co/G2cSINaTQo

In 2022, @PowerUSAID launched the Save Our Seas Initiative to combat ocean plastic pollution. @USAID's programs have prevented the equivalent of more than 10 billion plastic bottles from leaking into the environment. https://t.co/quDtk4K8dI

We launched the End Plastic Pollution International Collaborative – a global partnership that brings together governments, civil society, and businesses to develop innovative solutions to reduce plastic pollution around the world. https://t.co/JDcIJDgQ0v

As #OurOcean kicks off, we're continuing our work to tackle the climate crisis:

✅Supporting sustainable fishing practices
✅Conserving 30% of the global ocean by 2030
✅Combating marine pollution
✅Building sustainable blue economies
✅Bolstering maritime security

Here’s how: 🧵
Plastic pollution is everywhere. We’re working with 175 partners at the @UN to innovate real solutions and end plastic pollution. https://t.co/YyOfImGqub

We don't seek escalation, but we'll continue to support the defense of Israel and to protect our personnel in the region. - @SecBlinken on Iran’s unprecedented attack against Israel https://t.co/AF04prDLFf
.@SecBlinken and Iraqi Deputy Prime Minister Muhammad Ali Tamim co-chair a U.S.-Iraq Higher Coordinating Committee meeting at the State Department. https://t.co/azR1h1txGB https://t.co/3BMy7SF1Bj

This week @POTUS hosts Japanese Prime Minister Fumio Kishida for an official visit, we bring students and emerging leaders together for the Minority Serving Institutions conference, and a solar eclipse passes over North America. https://t.co/Ev2PodGvd7
Support for Ukraine is not just from the United States, it’s from country after country in Europe and well beyond. https://t.co/6yKSYGBt1E

Chef Masako Morishita operates one of Washington, D.C.’s most popular restaurants and also served as guest chef for the Official Visit luncheon honoring Japan’s Prime Minister. A member of the American Culinary Corps, here’s her take on cuisine rooted in her Japanese heritage. https://t.co/ySax3yCYOa
In a continuation of our strong partnership, two Japanese astronauts will join future American missions — and one will become the first non-American to land on the moon. https://t.co/k4IrD8a1Tc
.@SecBlinken delivers remarks at the second annual Minority Serving Institutions Conference at the Department of State. #MSIsAtState https://t.co/SL7CH49Qlw https://t.co/Wveo2FCZZw

The U.S. is working with Indo-Pacific partners to invest in each other’s economies.

Yesterday, @POTUS hosted the U.S.-Philippines-Japan Trilat and we announced the PGI Luzon Corridor in the Philippines to strengthen supply chains, food security, and our strategic partnership. https://t.co/xcQAm3oNGb

.@USAmbUN on the situation in Sudan: The world has largely remained silent and that must change and it has to change. The international community must give more, it must do more and it has to care more. https://t.co/ZOmhRwanRv

The U.S.-Japan Alliance is the cornerstone of peace and stability in the region, and America's commitment to Japan's defense is ironclad. The bonds between our nations and peoples are also ironclad. - @VP https://t.co/FwkmhX31AG
.@SecBlinken and @VP co-host and deliver remarks at a State luncheon in honor of Japanese Prime Minister Kishida Fumio. https://t.co/KBbdpUiJDc https://t.co/tx7bXiCIJM
The alliance between Japan and the United States is a cornerstone of peace, security, prosperity in the Indo-Pacific and around the world. - @POTUS https://t.co/KZ0TJfZOXs
During this week’s official visit, @POTUS and Japan’s Prime Minister Kishida will discuss efforts to strengthen our political, security, economic, and people-to-people ties. https://t.co/7lgevnTAAl

Ever wonder what goes into preparing for a State Dinner? Ahead of this week’s Official Visit by Japanese Prime Minister Kishida, we give you an interactive behind-the-scenes look: https://t.co/Ye3yXK8PlA
As Ramadan ends, we send warm wishes to all who are celebrating Eid al-Fitr. May your celebrations be filled with peace, blessings, and happiness. Eid Mubarak https://t.co/jZ5G1hjeRm

The flags are hung, the linens are set, and this week the red carpet will be rolled out as @POTUS welcomes Japan’s Prime Minister Kishida to Washington for an official visit. #StateOnTheStreet heads out to D.C.’s cherry blossoms for more. https://t.co/3niZvzGno0
.@SecBlinken holds a joint press availability with UK Foreign Secretary @David_Cameron. https://t.co/db7EgLtiJE https://t.co/ua13XqncAc

Today, Mexico, Canada, and the U.S. shared a rare astronomical event: a total solar eclipse. Watchers paused what they were doing to witness the special celestial moment. Our shared interest in space is a reminder that no matter where live, we are all under the same sun. https://t.co/coMqeurH6s
Follow a day in the life with @USNATO Ambassador Julie Smith as we celebrate 75 years of @NATO! https://t.co/F7c9HpK7Ka
Today we recall the 1994 genocide in Rwanda — known as Kwibuka 30 — and honor the victims. The United States remains steadfast in our support for the Rwandan people. https://t.co/Srdfqas4W7

This week, @SecBlinken traveled to Paris where he met with French President Macron and other officials to discuss our support for Ukraine, the conflict in Gaza, and stabilizing Haiti. He then headed to Belgium for the @NATO #ForMin and celebrated the Alliance’s 75th Anniversary. https://t.co/y0KdEI1yg3

The U.S. is a founding member of @NATO. From 12 original Allies to now 32, time and time again NATO has demonstrated that when we bind our nations together under the solemn oath to defend each other, we can deliver peace, security & stability for our citizens. @USNATO #WeAreNATO https://t.co/416zwLrWUo

Even as our Alliance changes, even as it evolves, its purpose remains enduring. Ours is a defensive Alliance. It’s never had and it never will have designs on the territory of any other country. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/gQhm04Xk7E

.@SecBlinken on @NATO: The purpose of this defensive Alliance is to allow us to get on with the real business of life, the real business of government, the real business of society: “achieving a fuller and happier life for all... our citizens.” https://t.co/V0H6LeBVzm

Earlier today @SecBlinken participated in a joint press availability on the EU-U.S. Trade and Technology Council in Belgium. “When the U.S. and the EU are aligned, when we’re working in common purpose, it’s a very powerful force.” https://t.co/h8zfbZmreH

As President Truman said at the founding, the purpose of the @NATO Alliance is to allow us to get on with the real business of life, the real business of government, the real business of society: Achieving a fuller and happier life for all our citizens. https://t.co/sHb7cRtxmO
75 years of security, support, and solidarity. NATO is stronger than ever. https://t.co/l32Wu3YUVX
Throughout its 75-year history, @NATO has played a vital role in collective security for its members. @USAmbNATO Julianne Smith reflects on NATO’s enduring legacy: https://t.co/RgAcgPaZew
.@SecBlinken holds a press availability in Brussels, Belgium. https://t.co/HKt1eGpaJ9 https://t.co/AGFheDJuSk
NATO is much more than a defensive alliance. It is helping to meet the humanitarian, medical, and infrastructure needs of today. https://t.co/7cOoMjfe1R

By expanding the @NATO Alliance, we have fortified the shield against aggression and brought more people under its protection. So that together we can focus on the real work of delivering for our people. https://t.co/UxT0GdvagG

For 75 years, @NATO has served as a shield against aggression, facilitating governments and society in achieving a fuller and happier life for all our citizens. Our latest digital story tracks how NATO’s contributions have contributed to our daily lives. https://t.co/kVlBy9DYoY
Our @NATO Alliance remains a bulwark of global security and stability — as it’s been for more than seven decades. https://t.co/jogngnNkcf

The United States and France are tackling global challenges together, including our shared efforts to end Russia’s brutal aggression against Ukraine, as well as working towards durable peace and security in the Middle East. https://t.co/w5jzdgnsVD

We’ve been grateful for France’s partnership on the crisis of the Middle East, and indeed working together to prevent the conflict that we see in Gaza from spreading to other parts of the region. — @SecBlinken https://t.co/atkJ60xVu3
.@SecBlinken participates in a joint press availability with French Foreign Minister @Steph_Sejourne in Paris, France. https://t.co/krgXooS9Yq https://t.co/BVcwimwX6m
Throughout April, we honor the contributions of Arab Americans to the United States in every field and profession, including diplomats here at the State Department. https://t.co/k7xBCxhLoz
